attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: ["Oxford University Press | Oxford University Press (OUP) is the largest university press in the world, and the second oldest after Cambridge University Press. It is a department of the University of Oxford and is governed by a group of 15 academics appointed by the vice-chancellor known as the delegates of the press. They are headed by the secretary to the delegates, who serves as OUP's chief executive and as its major representative on other university bodies. Oxford University has used a similar system to oversee OUP since the 17th century.", "University Press plc | University Press plc (UPPLC) is Nigeria's largest indigenous book publisher. It was founded as the Nigerian branch of the British Oxford University Press in 1949 with the name Oxford University Press (OUP), Nigeria. At incorporation as a public liability company in 1978, the company's name was changed to University Press Limited.", "Oxford Companions | Oxford Companions is a book series published by Oxford University Press, providing general knowledge within a specific area. The first book published in the series was \"The Oxford Companion to English Literature\" (1932), compiled by the retired diplomat Sir Paul Harvey."]

attributes.mlflow.spanInputs:
{"question": "what is the largest university press in the world that published Oxford Companions?", "trajectory": "[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
I need to find out which university press is the largest in the world and has published the Oxford Companions. This information might be available on Wikipedia, so I will search for it there.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "largest university press in the world that published Oxford Companions"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «Oxford University Press | Oxford University Press (OUP) is the largest university press in the world, and the second oldest after Cambridge University Press. It is a department of the University of Oxford and is governed by a group of 15 academics appointed by the vice-chancellor known as the delegates of the press. They are headed by the secretary to the delegates, who serves as OUP's chief executive and as its major representative on other university bodies. Oxford University has used a similar system to oversee OUP since the 17th century.»
[2] «University Press plc | University Press plc (UPPLC) is Nigeria's largest indigenous book publisher. It was founded as the Nigerian branch of the British Oxford University Press in 1949 with the name Oxford University Press (OUP), Nigeria. At incorporation as a public liability company in 1978, the company's name was changed to University Press Limited.»
[3] «Oxford Companions | Oxford Companions is a book series published by Oxford University Press, providing general knowledge within a specific area. The first book published in the series was "The Oxford Companion to English Literature" (1932), compiled by the retired diplomat Sir Paul Harvey.»"}

    next_tool_args = Field(annotation=dict[str, Any] required=True json_schema_extra={'__dspy_field_type': 'output', 'prefix': 'Next Tool Args:', 'desc': '${next_tool_args}'})
